name: Wall Jump
description: A jump launched from contact with a wall or steep vertical surface.
design_notes: Wall jump is part movement verb and part level-design contract. The player must be able to identify valid wall surfaces, understand the launch direction, and recover from missed timing. Decide whether the mechanic requires directional input away from the wall, permits neutral jumps, or uses an automatic rebound.
design_purpose: Create vertical routes and recovery options using level geometry.
Reward players who read walls as interactive surfaces instead of static barriers.
Support mastery through alternating wall contacts, jump timing, and air steering.
player_fantasy: The player feels nimble and resourceful, turning walls into launch points instead of obstacles.

parameters.name: wall_jump_horizontal_impulse
parameters.description: Launch push away from the contacted wall.

parameters.name: wall_jump_vertical_impulse
parameters.description: Upward launch component applied from wall contact.

parameters.name: wall_contact_grace_ms
parameters.description: Window after leaving a wall where the jump can still activate.

parameters.name: same_wall_lockout_ms
parameters.description: Delay before the same wall can be used again.

edge_cases: Inside corners can report two wall normals; choose the jump normal deterministically.
Moving, rotating, or crumbling walls must specify whether momentum is inherited.
Buffered jump after wall slide must not also trigger ground jump when the player lands during the buffer.
Wall jump near a ceiling needs head collision handling that does not destroy horizontal launch unfairly.
common_bugs: The wall normal is taken from the wrong contact point, launching the player into the wall.
Wall contact remains true for one extra physics step and grants jumps in open air.
Same-wall lockout prevents valid jumps on closely spaced opposite walls because contact IDs are reused.
Animation root motion fights the physics launch and shortens the jump.
balancing_notes: High horizontal impulse favors fast rebounds; low impulse favors controlled climbing and precision.
Generous contact grace makes wall jump approachable, but too much grace lets players jump from walls they no longer perceive.
accessibility_notes: Use surface marking, character pose, or controller vibration to show when a wall jump is available.
Avoid requiring simultaneous opposite-direction and jump inputs unless alternate input options exist.

implementation_notes.unity: Use an explicit movement-state enum and feed the final desired velocity into CharacterController.Move or Rigidbody movement from FixedUpdate; keep dash and jump assists outside animation callbacks. For Wall Jump, validate this path with parameters such as wall_jump_horizontal_impulse and wall_jump_vertical_impulse.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep the mechanic state on CharacterBody2D or CharacterBody3D, update velocity in _physics_process, and separate input buffering from collision response. For Wall Jump, validate this path with parameters such as wall_jump_horizontal_impulse and wall_jump_vertical_impulse.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use CharacterMovement with a custom movement state or Gameplay Ability; gate activation through prediction-safe input and reconcile movement corrections cleanly in networked play. For Wall Jump, validate this path with parameters such as wall_jump_horizontal_impulse and wall_jump_vertical_impulse.
implementation_notes.web: Represent the mechanic as deterministic state in the simulation loop, then apply resolved displacement before rendering through Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or a custom engine. For Wall Jump, validate this path with parameters such as wall_jump_horizontal_impulse and wall_jump_vertical_impulse.

relationships.reason: Wall slide can stabilize contact and timing before a wall jump impulse is accepted.
Wall jump gives wall run a clean exit action and expands route composition.
Small contact grace windows make wall jump inputs more forgiving around corners and uneven surfaces.

example_games.title: Super Meat Boy
example_games.note: Uses wall jumping for fast precision traversal.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses wall jumps and wall contact as part of precision climbing routes.

example_games.title: Hollow Knight
example_games.note: Uses wall interaction to expand traversal after an upgrade.
